chunks.text: EECS B.S. Advising - EECS at Berkeley EECS B.S. Advising Cory Hall EECS Undergraduate Advising & Support The Electrical Engineering Computer Science Division offers advising services for undergraduate students regarding degree requirements, enrollment, and resources. We are here to help and support you through your studies. Please feel free to contact us if you have any questions related to the EECS major, coursework, research, or just need someone to talk to. In This Page EECS Advising Calendar EECS vs. ESS Adviser EECS Major Advising EE Class Scheduling and Enrollment ESS Peer Advising Come talk to us about... Degree Requirements EECS Enrollment and waitlists EECS Honors Program Undergraduate Research Faculty Advising Tutoring and Academic support Drop-In Advising In-person Drop-in Advising availabile Tuesday/Wednesday 1:30 – 3:30 PM CS Students: 349 Soda EECS Students: 205 Cory Virtual Drop-in advising is available on Thursdays, 1:30 – 3:30 pm. Book an Appointment (Zoom) Need to chat with an advisor? Use our online booking system to schedule a 1:1 appointment. Book Zoom Appointment (EECS Only) You can always reach us during business hours by email, cs-advising@berkeley.edu (CS students) or eecs-advising@berkeley.edu (EECS students) EECS Advising Calendar (CalNet login required) EECS 101 | Ed Stem For the most up-to-date guidance on the EECS major, please join EECS 101 on EdStem . Contact Us Email: eecs-advising@berkeley.edu Make an Appointment For prospective students, schedule an appointment through BerkeleyEECS.YouCanBook.Me UC Berkeley students should use CalCentral to make appoinments. EECS Vs. ESS Use the table below to determine whether an EECS or ESS adviser is better suited to support your specific advising needs. EECS Department College of Engineering A.K.A. EECS Adviser or Major Adviser ESS Adviser or College adviser Location 205 Cory Hall 120 Grimes Engineering Center Experts on Anything related to the EECS major or minor EECS major and non-major related questions, college policies Questions to Ask – Program Planning – Ethics requirement – Enrollment questions – EE and CS course information – Undergraduate Research Internships – Graduate school planning Department programs (honors programs, student organizations, workshops, etc) – EECS Minor – Curricular Practical Training (CPT – Degree checks – H/SS – Withdrawing & Readmission – Double majors, etc – Program Planning – Petitions – Course units outside of the usual load – Optional Practical Training (OPT) – Exception petitions for EECS requirements – Study abroad EECS Advisors Gina Garcia EECS Student Advisor 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-7644 eecs-advising @ berkeley.edu EECS and Prospective students: Book an appointment with Gina Kait Guthrie EECS Student Advisor 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-1786 eecs-advising @ berkeley.edu EECS and Prospective students: Book an appointment with Kait Grayson Johnston eECS Student Advisor 205 Cory, +1 (510) 642-7372 eecs-advising @ berkeley.edu EECS and Prospective students: Book an

Peer advisers also meet with prospective students and lead workshops throughout each semester. Meet the peer advisers View Open Faculty Positions
